---
title: "SparCC_Networks"
author: "Elad Deiss-Yehiely"
date: "2026-04-08"
output: html_document
---

#Loading in files and libraries required. 
```{r}
### Note that the beginning work has been done in Wynton, which primarily was getting the network. Here we will be running through different correlations and viewing the images. ###

# ── Dependencies ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
library(pacman)
library(cowplot)
pacman::p_load(phyloseq, igraph, ggplot2, dplyr, tibble, Matrix, pals, vegan, stringr, tidyr)

source("/Users/ediessy/Desktop/Postdoc UCSF/CANOE/SparCC/bacterial_networks_April2026/bacterial_networks_func.R")

# ── Paths ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
base_dir_sparcc    <- "/Users/ediessy/Desktop/Postdoc UCSF/CANOE/SparCC/bacterial_networks_April2026"
sparcc_rds  <- file.path(base_dir_sparcc, "canoe_sparcc_base.rds")
phyloseq_rds_sparcc <- "/Users/ediessy/Desktop/Postdoc UCSF/CANOE/SparCC/bacterial_networks_April2026/Prevalence_Phyloseq_10_10.rds"

# ── Load phyloseq object ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
canoe_phy_sparcc <- readRDS(phyloseq_rds_sparcc)
```

```{r}
# Add SampleName column — required by the function
metadata_sparcc <- get_variable(canoe_phy_sparcc)
metadata_sparcc$SampleName <- rownames(metadata_sparcc)
sample_data(canoe_phy_sparcc) <- sample_data(metadata_sparcc)

# Rename taxa to match what was used when SparCC was run
taxa_names(canoe_phy_sparcc) <- paste0('ASV_', 1:length(taxa_names(canoe_phy_sparcc)))

message("Phyloseq object loaded: ", 
        nsamples(canoe_phy_sparcc), " samples, ", 
        ntaxa(canoe_phy_sparcc), " taxa")

# ── Threshold sweep ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
thresholds <- c(0.30, 0.35, 0.40, 0.45, 0.50)
results_list_sparcc <- vector("list", length(thresholds))

for (i in seq_along(thresholds)) {
  thresh     <- thresholds[i]
  thresh_str <- sprintf("%.2f", thresh)
  
  message("\n========================================")
  message("Running threshold: ", thresh_str)
  message("========================================")
  
  results_list_sparcc[[i]] <- generate_bacterial_modules(
    sparcc_rds_path      = sparcc_rds,
    phyloseq_obj         = canoe_phy_sparcc,
    cor_threshold        = thresh,
    png_output_path      = file.path(base_dir_sparcc, sprintf("Network_Modules_%s.png",   thresh_str)),
    eigenvector_csv_path = file.path(base_dir_sparcc, sprintf("Eigenvectors_%s.csv",      thresh_str)),
    asv_module_map_path  = file.path(base_dir_sparcc, sprintf("ASV2Modules_%s.csv",       thresh_str)),
    min_module_size      = 3,
    seed                 = 10
  )
  results_list_sparcc[[i]]$threshold <- thresh
}

# ── Comparison summary ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
summarise_result <- function(res) {
  mod_table    <- res$module_table
  module_sizes <- table(mod_table$Module)
  n_edges      <- igraph::ecount(res$graph)
  n_asvs_total <- ntaxa(canoe_phy_sparcc)
  
  tibble(
    threshold        = res$threshold,
    n_modules        = length(module_sizes),
    mean_module_size = round(mean(module_sizes), 1),
    median_mod_size  = median(module_sizes),
    max_module_size  = max(module_sizes),
    n_edges          = n_edges,
    n_asvs_assigned  = nrow(mod_table),
    pct_asv_assigned = round(nrow(mod_table) / n_asvs_total * 100, 1)
  )
}

comparison_df <- bind_rows(lapply(results_list_sparcc, summarise_result))

message("\n── Threshold Sensitivity Summary ──────────────────")
print(as.data.frame(comparison_df))

write.csv(comparison_df,
          file.path(base_dir_sparcc, "threshold_sensitivity_comparison.csv"),
          row.names = FALSE)

# ── Comparison plots ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_mods <- ggplot(comparison_df, aes(x = factor(threshold), y = n_modules)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#4E79A7") +
  geom_text(aes(label = n_modules), vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  labs(title = "Number of Modules", x = "SparCC Threshold", y = "N Modules") +
  theme_bw()

p_asv <- ggplot(comparison_df, aes(x = factor(threshold), y = pct_asv_assigned)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#F28E2B") +
  geom_text(aes(label = paste0(pct_asv_assigned, "%")), vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  labs(title = "% ASVs Assigned to a Module", x = "SparCC Threshold", y = "% Assigned") +
  ylim(0, 110) +
  theme_bw()

p_size <- ggplot(comparison_df, aes(x = factor(threshold), y = mean_module_size)) +
  geom_col(fill = "#59A14F") +
  geom_text(aes(label = mean_module_size), vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  labs(title = "Mean Module Size (ASVs)", x = "SparCC Threshold", y = "Mean Size") +
  theme_bw()

p_edges <- ggplot(comparison_df, aes(x = threshold, y = n_edges, group = 1)) +
  geom_line(color = "#E15759", linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3, color = "#E15759") +
  geom_text(aes(label = n_edges), vjust = -0.8, size = 3.2) +
  labs(title = "Network Edges vs. Threshold", x = "SparCC Threshold", y = "N Edges") +
  theme_bw()

comparison_plot <- cowplot::plot_grid(p_mods, p_asv, p_size, p_edges, ncol = 2)

comparison_plot

ggsave(file.path(base_dir_sparcc, "threshold_sensitivity_panel.png"),
       comparison_plot,
       width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 150)

message("\nDone.")
message("Summary table: threshold_sensitivity_comparison.csv")
message("Diagnostic plot: threshold_sensitivity_panel.png")

```

```{r}
### Chose 0.35 as the correlation coefficient to move forward with, will now take an image of the network. ###
# ── Enhanced network plot with Genus_ASV labels for threshold 0.35 ────────────

# Pull the 0.35 result from your sweep list
res_035 <- results_list_sparcc[[2]]   # index 2 = 0.35 threshold

graph_035    <- res_035$graph
modules_035  <- res_035$modules
module_table <- res_035$module_table

# ── Build Genus_ASV label for each vertex ─────────────────────────────────────
tax <- as.data.frame(tax_table(canoe_phy_sparcc))
tax$ASV <- rownames(tax)

# Create label: Genus_ASV_X (use Family if Genus is NA, Order if both NA)
tax$label <- paste0(
  ifelse(!is.na(tax$Genus)  & tax$Genus  != "", tax$Genus,
  ifelse(!is.na(tax$Family) & tax$Family != "", tax$Family,
  ifelse(!is.na(tax$Order)  & tax$Order  != "", tax$Order, "Unknown"))),
  "_", tax$ASV
)

# Match labels to vertices in the graph (only ASVs that passed degree filter)
vertex_names  <- igraph::V(graph_035)$name
vertex_labels <- tax$label[match(vertex_names, tax$ASV)]

# ── Colour by module ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
mods               <- sort(unique(igraph::V(graph_035)$module))
module_colors      <- pals::polychrome(length(mods))
module_colors_named <- setNames(module_colors, mods)
vertex_cols        <- module_colors_named[as.character(igraph::V(graph_035)$module)]

# ── Plot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
png(filename = file.path(base_dir_sparcc, "Network_Modules_0.35_labeled.png"),
    width = 6000, height = 5000, res = 400)

par(mar = c(2, 2, 2, 8))   # extra right margin for legend

plot(
  graph_035,
  vertex.color  = vertex_cols,
  vertex.size   = 8,
  edge.width    = 1.2,
  vertex.label  = vertex_labels,
  vertex.label.cex   = 0.35,       # adjust smaller/larger to taste
  vertex.label.dist  = 0.8,        # offset label from node center
  vertex.label.color = "black",
  layout        = igraph::layout_with_fr(graph_035)  # Fruchterman-Reingold
)

legend(
  "topright",
  legend = paste0("Module ", mods),
  col    = module_colors,
  pch    = 19, pt.cex = 1.5, cex = 0.7,
  bty    = "n", title = "Bacterial Modules", ncol = 1
)

dev.off()

message("Labeled network plot saved.")

### If you just want to see the plot itself run this: ###
layout_035 <- igraph::layout_with_fr(graph_035)

{
  plot(
    graph_035,
    vertex.color       = vertex_cols,
    vertex.size        = 8,
    edge.width         = 1.2,
    vertex.label       = vertex_labels,
    vertex.label.cex   = 0.35,
    vertex.label.dist  = 0.8,
    vertex.label.color = "black",
    layout             = layout_035
  )
  legend(
    "topright",
    legend = paste0("Module ", mods),
    col    = module_colors,
    pch    = 19, pt.cex = 1.5, cex = 0.7,
    bty    = "n", title = "Bacterial Modules", ncol = 1
  )
}
```

```{r}
### Chose 0.4 as the correlation coefficient to move forward with, will now take an image of the network. ###
# ── Enhanced network plot with Genus_ASV labels for threshold 0.35 ────────────

# Pull the 0.35 result from your sweep list
res_040 <- results_list_sparcc[[3]]   # index 2 = 0.35 threshold

graph_040    <- res_040$graph
modules_040  <- res_040$modules
module_table <- res_040$module_table

# ── Build Genus_ASV label for each vertex ─────────────────────────────────────
tax <- as.data.frame(tax_table(canoe_phy_sparcc))
tax$ASV <- rownames(tax)

# Create label: Genus_ASV_X (use Family if Genus is NA, Order if both NA)
tax$label <- paste0(
  ifelse(!is.na(tax$Genus)  & tax$Genus  != "", tax$Genus,
  ifelse(!is.na(tax$Family) & tax$Family != "", tax$Family,
  ifelse(!is.na(tax$Order)  & tax$Order  != "", tax$Order, "Unknown"))),
  "_", tax$ASV
)

# Match labels to vertices in the graph (only ASVs that passed degree filter)
vertex_names  <- igraph::V(graph_040)$name
vertex_labels <- tax$label[match(vertex_names, tax$ASV)]

# ── Colour by module ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
mods               <- sort(unique(igraph::V(graph_040)$module))
module_colors      <- pals::polychrome(length(mods))
module_colors_named <- setNames(module_colors, mods)
vertex_cols        <- module_colors_named[as.character(igraph::V(graph_040)$module)]

# ── Plot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
png(filename = file.path(base_dir_sparcc, "Network_Modules_0.40_labeled.png"),
    width = 6000, height = 5000, res = 400)

par(mar = c(2, 2, 2, 8))   # extra right margin for legend

plot(
  graph_040,
  vertex.color  = vertex_cols,
  vertex.size   = 8,
  edge.width    = 1.2,
  vertex.label  = vertex_labels,
  vertex.label.cex   = 0.35,       # adjust smaller/larger to taste
  vertex.label.dist  = 0.8,        # offset label from node center
  vertex.label.color = "black",
  layout        = igraph::layout_with_fr(graph_040)  # Fruchterman-Reingold
)

legend(
  "topright",
  legend = paste0("Module ", mods),
  col    = module_colors,
  pch    = 19, pt.cex = 1.5, cex = 0.7,
  bty    = "n", title = "Bacterial Modules", ncol = 1
)

dev.off()

message("Labeled network plot saved.")

### If you just want to see the plot itself run this: ###
layout_040 <- igraph::layout_with_fr(graph_040)

{
  plot(
    graph_040,
    vertex.color       = vertex_cols,
    vertex.size        = 8,
    edge.width         = 1.2,
    vertex.label       = vertex_labels,
    vertex.label.cex   = 0.35,
    vertex.label.dist  = 0.8,
    vertex.label.color = "black",
    layout             = layout_040
  )
  legend(
    "topright",
    legend = paste0("Module ", mods),
    col    = module_colors,
    pch    = 19, pt.cex = 1.5, cex = 0.7,
    bty    = "n", title = "Bacterial Modules", ncol = 1
  )
}

```


```{r}
# ── Also print a text summary of each module's members ───────────────────────
for (mod in sort(unique(module_table$Module))) {
  asvs <- module_table$ASV[module_table$Module == mod]
  genera <- tax$label[match(asvs, tax$ASV)]
  message("\nModule ", mod, " (", length(asvs), " ASVs):")
  message(paste(" -", genera, collapse = "\n"))
}

```

```{r}
#Neater image production of bacteria modules
library(ggraph)
library(tidygraph)
library(RColorBrewer)

# ── Prep taxonomy for vertices ─────────────────────────────────────────────────
tax <- as.data.frame(tax_table(canoe_phy_sparcc))
tax$ASV <- rownames(tax)

# Fill missing Family with Order, then Class, then "Unknown"
tax$Family_clean <- ifelse(!is.na(tax$Family) & tax$Family != "", tax$Family,
                    ifelse(!is.na(tax$Order)  & tax$Order  != "", paste0(tax$Order, " (Order)"),
                    ifelse(!is.na(tax$Class)  & tax$Class  != "", paste0(tax$Class, " (Class)"), "Unknown")))

# Genus_ASV label
tax$label <- paste0(
  ifelse(!is.na(tax$Genus) & tax$Genus != "", tax$Genus, tax$Family_clean),
  "\n", tax$ASV
)

# ── Convert igraph to tidygraph and annotate vertices ─────────────────────────
tg <- as_tbl_graph(graph_035) %>%
  activate(nodes) %>%
  mutate(
    degree       = igraph::degree(graph_035),
    module       = as.factor(igraph::V(graph_035)$module),
    ASV          = igraph::V(graph_035)$name,
    Family       = tax$Family_clean[match(igraph::V(graph_035)$name, tax$ASV)],
    genus_label  = tax$label[match(igraph::V(graph_035)$name, tax$ASV)]
  )

# ── Color palette by Family ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
families      <- sort(unique(igraph::V(graph_035) %>% 
                   { tax$Family_clean[match(igraph::V(graph_035)$name, tax$ASV)] }))
n_families    <- length(families)

# Use polychrome for many families, or Set1/Dark2 if fewer
family_colors <- setNames(
  pals::polychrome(max(n_families, 8))[1:n_families],
  families
)

# ── Publication-quality ggraph plot ───────────────────────────────────────────
set.seed(481516)

p_sparcc <- ggraph(tg, layout = "fr") +

  # Edges — bold, semi-transparent
  geom_edge_link(
    color     = "grey40",
    width     = 1.2,
    alpha     = 0.7
  ) +

  # Nodes — sized by degree, colored by Family
  geom_node_point(
    aes(color = Family, size = degree),
    alpha = 0.95
  ) +

  # Labels — only label genera with degree > 1 to reduce clutter
  geom_node_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(degree > 1, genus_label, "")),
    size        = 2.8,
    fontface    = "bold",
    repel       = TRUE,          # avoid label overlap
    max.overlaps = 20,
    point.padding = 0.3,
    box.padding   = 0.4,
    segment.color = "grey60",
    segment.size  = 0.3
  ) +

  # Module hull outlines — draws a convex hull around each module
  ggforce::geom_mark_hull(
    aes(x = x, y = y, group = module, label = paste0("Module ", module)),
    color       = "grey30",
    fill        = NA,
    linewidth   = 0.4,
    linetype    = "dashed",
    label.fontsize = 9,
    label.fontface = "bold",
    label.fill  = alpha("white", 0.7),
    expand      = unit(4, "mm"),
    con.size    = 0.3
  ) +

  scale_color_manual(values = family_colors, name = "Family") +
  scale_size_continuous(range = c(3, 10), name = "Node Degree") +

  labs(
    title    = "Bacterial Co-occurrence Network — CANOE Airway",
    subtitle = "SparCC threshold = 0.35 | Modules detected via fast-greedy algorithm",
    caption  = "Node size = degree centrality | Node color = Family | Dashed hulls = modules"
  ) +

  theme_graph(base_family = "Helvetica") +
  theme(
    plot.title    = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold",   hjust = 0.5),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, color = "grey40", hjust = 0.5),
    plot.caption  = element_text(size = 9,  color = "grey50"),
    legend.title  = element_text(size = 11, face = "bold"),
    legend.text   = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.margin   = ggplot2::margin(10, 10, 10, 10)
  )

# ── View in RStudio first ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────
p_sparcc

# ── Save high resolution once happy ──────────────────────────────────────────
ggsave(
  filename = file.path(base_dir_sparcc, "Network_Modules_0.35_publication.png"),
  plot     = p_sparcc,
  width    = 14,
  height   = 10,
  dpi      = 400,
  bg       = "white"
)
```


```{r}
#Now will try and associate modules to primary outcomes of interest.
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(dplyr)
library(broom.mixed)

eig_df <- read.csv(file.path(base_dir_sparcc, "Eigenvectors_0.35.csv"))
rownames(eig_df) <- eig_df$SampleName
eig_df$SampleName <- NULL

# ── Load metadata from phyloseq object ───────────────────────────────────────
metadata <- as.data.frame(sample_data(canoe_phy_sparcc))
metadata$SampleName <- rownames(metadata)

# ── Align samples between eigenvectors and metadata ──────────────────────────
common_samples <- intersect(rownames(eig_df), rownames(metadata))

message("Samples in eigenvectors: ", nrow(eig_df))
message("Samples in metadata:     ", nrow(metadata))
message("Common samples:          ", length(common_samples))

eig_aligned  <- eig_df[common_samples, , drop = FALSE]
meta_aligned <- metadata[common_samples, , drop = FALSE]
meta_aligned <- as.data.frame(meta_aligned)

# ── Confirm module columns ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
module_cols <- colnames(eig_aligned)
message("Modules available: ", paste(module_cols, collapse = ", "))

#valid_outcomes was defined earlier in the MaAsLiN3 code block but if you haven't run that section, just redefine it directly:
#── Define all primary outcomes ───────────────────────────────────────────────
primary_outcomes <- c(
  "wheeze12m", "chronic_rhinitis12m", "atopic_dermatitis12m", "food_allergy12m",
  "wheeze24m", "wheeze24m_current", "chronic_rhinitis24m", "chronic_rhinitis24m_current",
  "atopic_dermatitis24m", "atopic_dermatitis24m_current",
  "food_allergy24m", "food_allergy24m_current",
  "wheeze36m", "wheeze36m_current", "chronic_rhinitis36m", "chronic_rhinitis36m_current",
  "atopic_dermatitis36m", "atopic_dermatitis36m_current",
  "food_allergy36m", "food_allergy36m_current"
)

# ── Filter to outcomes that actually exist in your metadata with enough data ──
valid_outcomes <- primary_outcomes[sapply(primary_outcomes, function(x) {
  x %in% colnames(meta_aligned) && sum(!is.na(meta_aligned[[x]])) >= 20
})]

message("Valid outcomes found: ", length(valid_outcomes))
print(valid_outcomes)

# ── Combine eigenvectors and metadata into one dataframe ─────────────────────
module_cols <- colnames(eig_aligned)

meta_aligned <- data.frame(meta_aligned, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)

# Then this will work without any further wrapping
analysis_df <- bind_cols(
  eig_aligned,
  meta_aligned[, c(
    "Subject_ID",
    "Group", 
    "canoe_site.x",
    #"child...2.months_hhc_c_02a1",
    valid_outcomes
  )]
)

# Sanity check
message("analysis_df dimensions: ", nrow(analysis_df), " x ", ncol(analysis_df))
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
# ── Run mixed effects logistic regression per module per outcome ──────────────
# outcome ~ module_eigenvector + timepoint + Site + siblings + (1|SubjectID)

# ── Scale eigenvectors ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
eig_aligned_scaled <- as.data.frame(
  scale(eig_aligned, center = TRUE, scale = TRUE)
)
rownames(eig_aligned_scaled) <- rownames(eig_aligned)

message("Module means after scaling (should all be ~0):")
print(round(colMeans(eig_aligned_scaled), 3))
message("Module SDs after scaling (should all be ~1):")
print(round(apply(eig_aligned_scaled, 2, sd), 3))

# ── Build analysis dataframe ──────────────────────────────────────────────────
analysis_df <- bind_cols(
  eig_aligned_scaled,
  data.frame(meta_aligned[, c(
    "Subject_ID",
    "Group",
    "canoe_site.x",
    #"child...2.months_hhc_c_02a1",
    valid_outcomes
  )], stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
)

analysis_df$Group <- analysis_df$Group <- as.factor(analysis_df$Group)

# ── Initialize results list ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
results_list <- list()

# ── GLMER loop ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
for (outcome in valid_outcomes) {
  for (module in module_cols) {
    
    formula_str <- sprintf(
      "%s ~ %s + Group + canoe_site.x + (1|Subject_ID)",
      outcome, module
    )
    
    # Single clean model_df definition
    model_df <- analysis_df %>%
      filter(!is.na(.data[[outcome]])) %>%
      mutate(!!outcome := as.integer(.data[[outcome]]))
    
    fit <- tryCatch(
      glmer(
        as.formula(formula_str),
        data    = model_df,
        family  = binomial(link = "logit"),
        control = glmerControl(
          optimizer = "bobyqa",
          optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5)
        )
      ),
      error = function(e) {
        message("Model failed for ", outcome, " ~ ", module, ": ", e$message)
        return(NULL)
      }
    )
    
    if (is.null(fit)) next
    
    coef_table <- broom.mixed::tidy(fit, effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE)
    module_row <- coef_table %>% filter(term == module)
    
    if (nrow(module_row) == 0) next
    
    results_list[[paste(outcome, module, sep = "__")]] <- tibble(
      outcome   = outcome,
      module    = module,
      estimate  = module_row$estimate,
      std_error = module_row$std.error,
      statistic = module_row$statistic,
      pval      = module_row$p.value,
      conf_low  = module_row$conf.low,
      conf_high = module_row$conf.high
    )
  }
}

# ── Combine, FDR correct, save ────────────────────────────────────────────────
all_results <- bind_rows(results_list)
all_results$qval <- p.adjust(all_results$pval, method = "BH")

sig_results <- all_results %>%
  filter(qval < 0.1) %>%
  arrange(qval)

message("Total tests run: ",                       nrow(all_results))
message("Significant associations (FDR < 0.1): ", nrow(sig_results))
print(sig_results)

# outdir <- file.path(base_dir_sparcc, "GLMER_results")
# dir.create(outdir, showWarnings = FALSE)
# 
# write.csv(all_results, file.path(outdir, "all_results.csv"),         row.names = FALSE)
# write.csv(sig_results, file.path(outdir, "significant_results.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
# 
# message("Results saved to: ", outdir)
```


```{r}
### THINKING ABOUT IN TERMS OF JUST ONE OUTCOME TIMEPOINT, PIVOT TO WIDE FORMAT ###
# ── Define 36m outcomes ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
outcomes_36m <- c(
  "chronic_rhinitis12m", "chronic_rhinitis24m",
  "chronic_rhinitis36m"
)

valid_outcomes_36m <- outcomes_36m[sapply(outcomes_36m, function(x) {
  x %in% colnames(meta_aligned) && sum(!is.na(meta_aligned[[x]])) >= 20
})]

# ── Reshape to wide format — one row per subject ──────────────────────────────
eig_wide <- eig_aligned_scaled %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Subject_ID = meta_aligned$Subject_ID,
    Group      = meta_aligned$Group
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    id_cols     = Subject_ID,
    names_from  = Group,
    values_from = all_of(module_cols),
    names_sep   = "__"
  )

message("Wide format: ", nrow(eig_wide), " subjects x ", ncol(eig_wide), " columns")

# ── Add outcomes and site — one row per subject ───────────────────────────────
subject_meta <- meta_aligned %>%
  data.frame(stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Subject_ID) %>%
  dplyr::slice(1) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::select(Subject_ID, canoe_site.x, all_of(valid_outcomes_36m))

analysis_wide <- eig_wide %>%
  dplyr::left_join(subject_meta, by = "Subject_ID")

message("Final wide dataframe: ", nrow(analysis_wide), " x ", ncol(analysis_wide))

# ── Clean column names ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
colnames(analysis_wide) <- gsub(" ", "_", colnames(analysis_wide))
colnames(analysis_wide) <- gsub("-", "_", colnames(analysis_wide))

predictor_cols <- grep("Module", colnames(analysis_wide), value = TRUE)
message("Predictor columns (", length(predictor_cols), "):")
print(predictor_cols)

valid_outcomes_36m_clean <- valid_outcomes_36m[
  valid_outcomes_36m %in% colnames(analysis_wide)
]

# ── GLM loop ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
results_list <- list()

for (outcome in valid_outcomes_36m_clean) {
  for (predictor in predictor_cols) {
    
    parts  <- strsplit(predictor, "__")[[1]]
    module <- parts[1]
    visit  <- paste(parts[-1], collapse = "__")
    
    formula_str <- sprintf("%s ~ %s + canoe_site.x", outcome, predictor)
    
    model_df <- analysis_wide %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[outcome]])) %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[predictor]])) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(!!outcome := as.integer(.data[[outcome]]))
    
    if (sum(model_df[[outcome]], na.rm = TRUE) < 20) next
    
    fit <- tryCatch(
      glm(as.formula(formula_str), data = model_df, family = binomial(link = "logit")),
      error = function(e) { message("Failed: ", outcome, " ~ ", predictor); return(NULL) }
    )
    
    if (is.null(fit)) next
    
    coef_table <- broom::tidy(fit, conf.int = TRUE)
    pred_row   <- coef_table %>% dplyr::filter(term == predictor)
    if (nrow(pred_row) == 0) next
    
    results_list[[paste(outcome, predictor, sep = "__")]] <- tibble(
      outcome   = outcome,
      module    = module,
      visit     = visit,
      estimate  = pred_row$estimate,
      std_error = pred_row$std.error,
      statistic = pred_row$statistic,
      pval      = pred_row$p.value,
      conf_low  = pred_row$conf.low,
      conf_high = pred_row$conf.high
    )
  }
}

# ── FDR correct and save ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
all_results <- dplyr::bind_rows(results_list)
all_results$qval <- p.adjust(all_results$pval, method = "BH")

sig_results <- all_results %>%
  dplyr::filter(qval < 0.1) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(qval)

message("Total tests run: ",                       nrow(all_results))
message("Significant associations (FDR < 0.1): ", nrow(sig_results))
print(sig_results)

# outdir <- file.path(base_dir_sparcc, "GLMER_results_36m_wide")
# dir.create(outdir, showWarnings = FALSE)
# write.csv(all_results, file.path(outdir, "all_results.csv"),         row.names = FALSE)
# write.csv(sig_results, file.path(outdir, "significant_results.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
# message("Results saved to: ", outdir)
```

---

## What This Now Tests

Each model is a clean logistic regression:
```
food_allergy36m ~ Module4_score_at_newborn + site
food_allergy36m ~ Module4_score_at_4months + site
food_allergy36m ~ Module4_score_at_12months + site



```
#This one includees read depth as a co-variate
```{r}
# This must be done BEFORE the pivot
read_depth <- data.frame(
  read_depth = sample_sums(canoe_phy_sparcc)
)
read_depth$SampleName <- rownames(read_depth)

# Add read depth to meta_aligned
meta_aligned$read_depth <- read_depth$read_depth[
  match(rownames(meta_aligned), read_depth$SampleName)
]

# Confirm it looks right
summary(meta_aligned$read_depth)
# Should show realistic sequencing depths e.g. min ~1000, max ~100000

# ── Reshape to wide format — one row per subject ──────────────────────────────
eig_wide <- eig_aligned_scaled %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    Subject_ID = meta_aligned$Subject_ID,
    Group      = meta_aligned$Group,
    read_depth = meta_aligned$read_depth   # include read depth here before pivot
  ) %>%
  tidyr::pivot_wider(
    id_cols     = Subject_ID,
    names_from  = Group,
    values_from = c(all_of(module_cols), "read_depth"),  # pivot both modules and read depth
    names_sep   = "__"
  )

message("Wide format: ", nrow(eig_wide), " subjects x ", ncol(eig_wide), " columns")

# Confirm read depth columns were created
rd_cols <- grep("read_depth", colnames(eig_wide), value = TRUE)
message("Read depth columns: ", paste(rd_cols, collapse = ", "))

# ── Add outcomes and site — one row per subject ───────────────────────────────
subject_meta <- meta_aligned %>%
  data.frame(stringsAsFactors = FALSE) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Subject_ID) %>%
  dplyr::slice(1) %>%
  dplyr::ungroup() %>%
  dplyr::select(Subject_ID, canoe_site.x, all_of(valid_outcomes_36m))

analysis_wide <- eig_wide %>%
  dplyr::left_join(subject_meta, by = "Subject_ID")

message("Final wide dataframe: ", nrow(analysis_wide), " x ", ncol(analysis_wide))

# ── Clean column names ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
colnames(analysis_wide) <- gsub(" ", "_", colnames(analysis_wide))
colnames(analysis_wide) <- gsub("-", "_", colnames(analysis_wide))

# ── Confirm predictor and read depth columns after cleaning ───────────────────
predictor_cols <- grep("^Module", colnames(analysis_wide), value = TRUE)
rd_cols_clean  <- grep("read_depth", colnames(analysis_wide), value = TRUE)

message("Predictor columns (", length(predictor_cols), "):")
print(predictor_cols)
message("Read depth columns (", length(rd_cols_clean), "):")
print(rd_cols_clean)

valid_outcomes_36m_clean <- valid_outcomes_36m[
  valid_outcomes_36m %in% colnames(analysis_wide)
]

# ── GLM loop — now includes matched read depth per visit ─────────────────────
results_list <- list()

for (outcome in valid_outcomes_36m_clean) {
  for (predictor in predictor_cols) {
    
    parts  <- strsplit(predictor, "__")[[1]]
    module <- parts[1]
    visit  <- paste(parts[-1], collapse = "__")
    
    # Match read depth column to the same visit as the predictor
    # e.g. Module1__child__newborn → read_depth__child__newborn
    rd_col <- paste0("read_depth__", visit)
    
    # Skip if matching read depth column doesn't exist
    if (!rd_col %in% colnames(analysis_wide)) {
      message("No read depth column found for visit: ", visit, " — skipping")
      next
    }
    
    # Scale read depth — important since it can range 1000-100000
    formula_str <- sprintf(
      "%s ~ %s + canoe_site.x + scale(%s)",
      outcome, predictor, rd_col
    )
    
    model_df <- analysis_wide %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[outcome]])) %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[predictor]])) %>%
      dplyr::filter(!is.na(.data[[rd_col]])) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(!!outcome := as.integer(.data[[outcome]]))
    
    if (sum(model_df[[outcome]], na.rm = TRUE) < 20) next
    
    fit <- tryCatch(
      glm(
        as.formula(formula_str),
        data   = model_df,
        family = binomial(link = "logit")
      ),
      error = function(e) {
        message("Failed: ", outcome, " ~ ", predictor)
        return(NULL)
      }
    )
    
    if (is.null(fit)) next
    
    coef_table <- broom::tidy(fit, conf.int = TRUE)
    pred_row   <- coef_table %>% dplyr::filter(term == predictor)
    if (nrow(pred_row) == 0) next
    
    results_list[[paste(outcome, predictor, sep = "__")]] <- tibble(
      outcome   = outcome,
      module    = module,
      visit     = visit,
      estimate  = pred_row$estimate,
      std_error = pred_row$std.error,
      statistic = pred_row$statistic,
      pval      = pred_row$p.value,
      conf_low  = pred_row$conf.low,
      conf_high = pred_row$conf.high
    )
  }
}

# ── FDR correct and save ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
all_results <- dplyr::bind_rows(results_list)
all_results$qval <- p.adjust(all_results$pval, method = "BH")

sig_results <- all_results %>%
  dplyr::filter(qval < 0.1) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(qval) %>%
  dplyr::mutate(
    odds_ratio = round(exp(estimate), 3),
    or_low     = round(exp(conf_low),  3),
    or_high    = round(exp(conf_high), 3),
    temporality = dplyr::case_when(
      grepl("newborn",  visit) & grepl("12m", outcome) ~ "Prospective",
      grepl("newborn",  visit) & grepl("24m", outcome) ~ "Prospective",
      grepl("newborn",  visit) & grepl("36m", outcome) ~ "Prospective",
      grepl("4_months", visit) & grepl("24m", outcome) ~ "Prospective",
      grepl("4_months", visit) & grepl("36m", outcome) ~ "Prospective",
      grepl("12_months",visit) & grepl("36m", outcome) ~ "Prospective",
      TRUE ~ "Concurrent"
    )
  ) %>%
  dplyr::select(outcome, module, visit,
                estimate, std_error, odds_ratio, or_low, or_high,
                pval, qval, temporality)

message("Total tests run: ",                      nrow(all_results))
message("Significant associations (FDR < 0.1): ", nrow(sig_results))
message("\nProspective findings:")
print(sig_results %>% dplyr::filter(temporality == "Prospective"))
message("\nConcurrent findings:")
print(sig_results %>% dplyr::filter(temporality == "Concurrent"))

outdir <- file.path(base_dir_sparcc, "GLMER_results_36m_wide")
dir.create(outdir, showWarnings = FALSE)
write.csv(all_results, file.path(outdir, "all_results.csv"),         row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(sig_results, file.path(outdir, "significant_results.csv"), row.names = FALSE)
message("Results saved to: ", outdir)

```

```{r}
### TROUBLESHOOTING FOR VERY LOW PVALUES ####
# Replace with whichever outcome/module has qval ~ 0
# Check what the outcome actually looks like in your data
problem_outcome <- sig_results$outcome[1]

# Before encoding
cat("Before encoding:\n")
table(analysis_df[[problem_outcome]], useNA = "always")
cat("Class:", class(analysis_df[[problem_outcome]]), "\n")

# Simulate what the encoding does
encoded <- as.numeric(as.character(analysis_df[[problem_outcome]]))
cat("\nAfter encoding:\n")
table(encoded, useNA = "always")

sapply(valid_outcomes, function(x) {
  vals <- analysis_df[[x]]
  cat(x, "— class:", class(vals), 
      "| unique values:", paste(sort(unique(vals)), collapse = ", "), "\n")
})




analysis_df %>%
  dplyr::group_by(Subject_ID) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_rows        = n(),
    n_unique_wheeze = n_distinct(wheeze12m, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  dplyr::count(n_unique_wheeze)

```
